title: Fly Fishing From A Rowing Boat On The Emerald Waters Near El Bolson Rio Negro
description: El Bolsón, Río Negro: Samuel Jeffery and Daniel Bergner navigate the tranquil, glacial-fed waters in a classic wooden drift boat named 'Tucu Tucu'. The photograph captures a serene moment of focus as the fly rod arc slices through the crisp Patagonian air, emphasizing the rhythmic connection between the angler and the river's flow. Sunlight filters through the dense, verdant canopy of native coihue trees, casting soft highlights onto the teal surface of the water. The composition utilizes a shallow depth of field to isolate the boat against the rich, textured backdrop of the temperate rainforest. Every ripple in the water speaks to the undisturbed wilderness that defines this corner of Argentina, where the pace of life slows to match the current. Daniel Bergner maneuvers the oars with practiced ease, illustrating the quiet collaboration required for navigating these remote waterways. This image serves as a profound testament to the region's allure for outdoor enthusiasts and the deep, enduring traditions of sport fishing in Patagonia. The juxtaposition of the vibrant white boat against the deep emerald forest provides a striking visual narrative of human exploration within a pristine environment. This scene highlights how remote, untouched landscapes continue to anchor the cultural identity and tourism appeal of the Andean foothills. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
